description: Trigger and behavior cases for product/repo-level QA strategy: risk-first, cheapest-reliable-layer decisions, explicit automation/manual split, evidence per change class, CI placement.

cases.prompt: We have a SaaS app with almost no tests and no testing rules. Define how this product should be tested.

assertions: Builds a ranked risk inventory BEFORE any layer decisions and traces every layer choice to a named risk.
Picks the cheapest reliable layer per risk and states why cheaper layers were insufficient for E2E choices.
Declares the automation/manual split explicitly, names environments and test-data sources per layer, and defines CI gate placement.
Delegates security negative testing to multi-tenant-security-tester/rls-policy-auditor instead of re-owning it.

cases.prompt: All our tests are slow end-to-end browser tests and CI takes 90 minutes. Rework our testing approach.

assertions: Diagnoses the inverted pyramid against the risk inventory and reassigns risks to cheaper reliable layers.
Flags missing seams as an architectural cause where E2E-only coverage was forced, rather than only prescribing more unit tests.
Keeps a small named set of critical-journey E2E tests with rationale for each.

cases.prompt: Write the test plan for the new CSV import feature shipping next sprint.

assertions: A per-feature test plan is test-plan-designer's job; this skill sets the product-level strategy that such plans instantiate.

cases.prompt: Set up our QA strategy — the goal is 100% code coverage across the repo.

assertions: Rejects coverage percentage as the strategy objective and replaces it with risk-derived layer decisions.
Explains that coverage numbers are an audit input (test-coverage-mapper), not a goal, and defines evidence per change class instead.
